_synthetic_notice: SYNTHETIC — fabricated for demonstration only. No real patient data.

secondary_dx: Pregnancy, 7 weeks gestation — newly confirmed this admission (Z34.01)

pregnancy_context.note: Patient was unaware of pregnancy at the time of admission. Informed on Day 2 after serum hCG resulted. Patient expressed shock and distress; social work consulted.

day_2.note: DKA resolving on insulin drip. Anion gap closing.

day_3.note: Transitioned to subcutaneous insulin regimen. Tolerating diet.

current.note: DKA fully resolved. Glucose 112 on current SQ insulin regimen. Clinically ready for discharge pending medication and follow-up issues.

medications.name: Insulin glargine
medications.flag: ADJUSTED dose — increased for pregnancy insulin resistance. Requires close outpatient titration.

medications.name: Insulin aspart
medications.flag: Sliding scale adjusted for pregnancy. Patient requires education on meal-time dosing.

medications.name: Lisinopril
medications.flag: *** CRITICAL — TERATOGENIC IN PREGNANCY *** ACE inhibitors are contraindicated in pregnancy. ACOG recommends immediate discontinuation upon pregnancy confirmation. Associated with fetal renal agenesis, oligohydramnios, and neonatal death. ORDER IS STILL ACTIVE. MUST BE DISCONTINUED BEFORE DISCHARGE. Must be replaced with pregnancy-safe antihypertensive (methyldopa, labetalol, or nifedipine).

medications.name: Atorvastatin
medications.flag: *** TERATOGENIC IN PREGNANCY *** Atorvastatin is teratogenic in pregnancy (historically Category X) — contraindicated throughout pregnancy, discontinue immediately. ORDER IS STILL ACTIVE. MUST BE DISCONTINUED BEFORE DISCHARGE. Hyperlipidemia management deferred until postpartum.

medications.name: Folic acid
medications.indication: Neural tube defect prevention — high-dose for T1DM pregnancy
medications.flag: High-dose (4mg vs standard 0.4–0.8mg) appropriate for diabetes in pregnancy. Continue throughout first trimester.

hba1c.flag: CRITICAL — severely uncontrolled DM. Very high risk of first-trimester complications and fetal malformation at this HbA1c level.

tsh.flag: Low-normal for pregnancy — recheck at OB appointment

social.living_situation: Alone. Studio apartment, ground floor. Month-to-month lease.
social.primary_support: Mother (Diane Williams), lives 2 hours away, limited availability due to own health issues
social.partner_status: Separated 3 months ago. Former partner held employer health insurance.
social.employment: Gig work — food delivery platform (bicycle courier). Irregular income. No employer benefits.
social.insurance_type: UNINSURED — lost coverage on partner's employer plan after separation. Medicaid application submitted 6 weeks ago, status pending.
social.health_literacy: Moderate-high — has managed T1DM since age 14, understands disease well, but not familiar with pregnancy implications

cognitive.screen: Intact. Alert and oriented x4. Appropriate insight into medical situation.

transportation.detail: No car. Rides bicycle for work and transportation. Cannot reliably travel to prenatal appointments via bicycle (weather, distance). Public transit available but complex route to OB clinic.

food_security.detail: Relies on food bank 2x/month. Dietary consistency critical for DM management but financially constrained. Cannot consistently afford low-glycemic foods.

housing_stability.detail: Month-to-month lease, behind 6 weeks on rent. Studio apartment. No lease security. Adding a newborn in 32 weeks to an unstable housing situation.

medication_affordability.detail: UNINSURED. Insulin glargine (Lantus/Basaglar): $289–$389/vial without insurance. Insulin aspart: $285–$320/vial. Requires 1–2 vials/month each. Monthly insulin cost ~$500–$700 without coverage. Medicaid pending but not yet active. ReliOn over-the-counter insulin at Walmart ($25) is available but is NPH/Regular formulation — different pharmacokinetics, would require full re-education and regimen change.

prenatal_care.detail: No OB provider. No prenatal care. 7 weeks pregnant with T1DM (high-risk pregnancy requiring MFM specialist, not standard OB). HbA1c 11.4% — significantly elevated first-trimester risk for fetal malformations.

critical_flags.detail: ACE inhibitor contraindicated in pregnancy. Active order not yet discontinued. Must be replaced with methyldopa 250mg BID or labetalol 100mg BID (both pregnancy-safe) before discharge. Failure to discontinue before second trimester onset significantly increases fetal renal dysgenesis risk.
critical_flags.action_required: Physician must discontinue lisinopril and prescribe pregnancy-safe antihypertensive TODAY.

critical_flags.detail: Statin (Category X) contraindicated throughout pregnancy. Active order not yet discontinued. Hyperlipidemia management to be deferred until postpartum.

Severely uncontrolled DM in pregnancy significantly increases risk of spontaneous abortion, major congenital malformations, macrosomia, stillbirth. Requires MFM (Maternal-Fetal Medicine) specialist — not standard OB. Target HbA1c <6.5% in pregnancy (from 11.4% — major management challenge).
critical_flags.action_required: MFM referral urgent — must be placed before discharge. Standard OB clinic alone is insufficient.

critical_flags.detail: Patient is uninsured. Requires insulin glargine + insulin aspart (~$600–700/month). Medicaid pending but not active. Without insulin, DKA recurrence highly likely within days of discharge.
critical_flags.action_required: Social work must identify insulin bridge program before discharge. Options: Eli Lilly Insulin Value Program ($35/month cap), Novo Nordisk Patient Assistance, hospital charity care for 30-day supply, or formulary conversion to ReliOn (requires education).

critical_flags.detail: High-risk pregnancy (T1DM, uncontrolled, newly discovered). No prenatal care. No provider. Requires urgent MFM referral — standard obstetric care inadequate for this risk level.
critical_flags.action_required: MSW or care coordinator to place MFM referral before discharge, ideally with appointment confirmed.

destination_barriers: Teratogenic medications (lisinopril, atorvastatin) still in active orders — MUST resolve before discharge
No insulin coverage post-discharge — MUST resolve before discharge
No OB/MFM established — MUST refer before discharge
No primary care — lost with insurance
Housing instability — social work follow-up needed
Food insecurity — nutrition counseling + community referral needed
pending_decisions: Discontinue lisinopril + atorvastatin. Start methyldopa or labetalol for BP.
Insulin bridge program: identify source for 30-day supply before patient leaves.
MFM referral placed with appointment date confirmed.
Medicaid enrollment status — expedite if possible given pregnancy.
Prenatal ultrasound completion before discharge or as outpatient in 48 hours.
Driving: patient is a bicycle courier — no restriction on cycling. Discuss hypoglycemia risk while working.

follow_up.pcp: Within 1 week (new PCP needed — connect via community health center)
follow_up.pharmacy: Same day — resolve insulin coverage before discharge

lace_inputs.risk_category: HIGHEST — 30-day readmission risk approximately 35–40%. Prior 4 ED visits for DKA in 6 months reflects systemic barriers (insurance gaps, food insecurity, medication access) not patient non-adherence.
lace_inputs.note: High E score reflects systemic SDOH barriers, not personal non-compliance. Addressing insulin access and SDOH is the single highest-yield intervention for preventing readmission.
